title: VulnHunter-Fix worker result
description: Schema for per-finding worker result files (.work/<repo>/.vulnfix-manifests/group-NNN_result.json) and the aggregate .vulnfix_result.json. Covers Honesty (REQ-HON), graph-citation (REQ-GRA), and sweep (REQ-SWP) fields.

completenessTier.description: Terminal completeness classification per REQ-HON-001. LLM_REVIEW is intentionally excluded — it is an intermediate signal from the deterministic classifier, not a terminal state (REQ-HON-012, REQ-HON-013).

workerStatus.description: REQ-HON-011: VERIFIED may appear bare or with a tier suffix. All four VERIFIED_* forms carry success semantics; the tier is disclosed via completeness_tier. FAILED is emitted when the TDD cycle does not close after retries — semantically distinct from NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (finding too complex to auto-fix) vs CANNOT_AUTO_FIX (requires human coordination).

tierJudgment.description: Populated only when the deterministic classifier returned LLM_REVIEW and the bounded LLM fallback was invoked (REQ-HON-013 through REQ-HON-016).

residual_vectors.description: Per REQ-HON-005: non-empty when completeness_tier != FULL; empty only when tier == FULL. Cross-field constraint enforced below via allOf.

callers_routed_through_fix.description: REQ-GRA-019: list of file:symbol pointers that route through the safe pathway after this fix. Must be a superset of graph.callers_of(sink_symbol) for column 7 of the verification table to read 'yes' (REQ-GRA-013).

callers_not_routed.description: REQ-GRA-019: list of callers NOT routed through the fix, each with a reason. Non-empty when the sink has callers outside the fixed code path — those become residual vectors.

files_modified.description: Repo-relative paths modified by this fix. Consumed by scripts/check-scope.py (Gate 3, --files-modified) and scripts/sweep-root-causes.py Path A (sibling defects on files already in this list are amended into the PR rather than filed as follow-up issues).

discrimination_evidence.description: REQ-GRA-017: recorded at Step E.5 of the TDD cycle. Required non-null when completeness_tier is FULL AND status is VERIFIED/VERIFIED_FULL (a fix was applied and verified) — enforced by the allOf below. ALREADY_FIXED / FIX_NOT_NEEDED FULL results are exempt: there is no fix diff to discriminate.

sweep_ran.description: REQ-SWP-001: true if Sweep phase executed for this finding.

sweep_summary.description: REQ-SWP-003 five-column summary for this ROOT cause.

sweep_revised.description: REQ-SWP-006: true when Sweep downgraded tier from FULL to MITIGATION.

discrimination_evidence.description: S1 (12-seg review): a FULL+VERIFIED result must carry non-hollow proof. Require the method + both fix results, and constrain them to the only discriminating combination (pre=fail, post=pass) so the contract matches its consumer compute-completeness-tier.py.

then.description: S4 (12-seg review): the sweep sets sweep_revised=true when an unmitigated sibling remains (REQ-SWP-006). Such a finding CANNOT be FULL — it must downgrade to MITIGATION/WORKAROUND. This is the mechanical backstop that makes 'a found sibling cannot ship as FULL' true, not just prose.
